description: National Science Foundation proposal scoring rubric based on the two NSF merit review criteria. All proposals are evaluated on Intellectual Merit and Broader Impacts, weighted equally.

overall_scoring.description: Each category score is the weighted average of its sub-criteria. The overall score is the equally-weighted average of the two category scores.

intellectual_merit.name: Intellectual Merit
intellectual_merit.description: The Intellectual Merit criterion encompasses the potential to advance knowledge within and across fields.

sub_criteria.name: Research Question Clarity
sub_criteria.description: How clearly and precisely the research questions or hypotheses are articulated, including well-defined scope and expected contributions.

score_descriptors.1: Research questions are absent, vague, or incoherent. No clear direction for the proposed work.
score_descriptors.2: Research questions are poorly defined with significant ambiguity. Scope is unclear or overly broad.
score_descriptors.3: Research questions are stated but lack precision. Some aspects remain underdeveloped or loosely connected.
score_descriptors.4: Research questions are clearly articulated with well-defined scope. Minor gaps in specificity.
score_descriptors.5: Research questions are exceptionally precise, well-motivated, and demonstrate a deep understanding of the problem space.

sub_criteria.name: Innovation
sub_criteria.description: The degree to which the proposed work explores creative, original, or potentially transformative concepts and approaches.

score_descriptors.1: No novelty. The work is entirely derivative with no new ideas or approaches.
score_descriptors.2: Minimal innovation. Incremental extension of existing work with little creative contribution.
score_descriptors.3: Moderate innovation. Some novel elements but largely builds on established approaches.
score_descriptors.4: Highly innovative. Introduces new concepts or methods that advance the field meaningfully.
score_descriptors.5: Potentially transformative. Challenges existing paradigms and could fundamentally reshape understanding or practice.

sub_criteria.name: Methodology Rigor
sub_criteria.description: The soundness and appropriateness of the proposed research methods, analytical framework, and experimental design.

score_descriptors.1: Methods are absent or fundamentally flawed. No viable path to answering the research questions.
score_descriptors.2: Methods have significant weaknesses. Key controls, variables, or analytical steps are missing.
score_descriptors.3: Methods are generally sound but have notable gaps. Some aspects need further justification.
score_descriptors.4: Methods are well-designed and appropriate. Minor refinements could strengthen the approach.
score_descriptors.5: Methods are exemplary, thoroughly justified, and represent best practices. All potential issues are addressed.

sub_criteria.name: Feasibility
sub_criteria.description: The likelihood that the proposed work can be accomplished within the stated timeframe, budget, and available resources.

score_descriptors.1: The project is unrealistic given the proposed timeline, budget, or available resources.
score_descriptors.2: Major feasibility concerns. Critical resources or capabilities appear lacking.
score_descriptors.3: Generally feasible but some milestones or resource assumptions are questionable.
score_descriptors.4: Feasible with a realistic timeline and well-justified resource allocation. Minor risks identified.
score_descriptors.5: Highly feasible. Clear evidence of capacity, realistic milestones, and contingency planning.

sub_criteria.name: Preliminary Data
sub_criteria.description: The quality and relevance of preliminary results or prior work that supports the proposed research direction.

score_descriptors.1: No preliminary data provided. No evidence that the proposed approach is viable.
score_descriptors.2: Minimal or tangentially related preliminary data. Weak support for the proposed direction.
score_descriptors.3: Some relevant preliminary data but gaps remain. Partial support for feasibility.
score_descriptors.4: Strong preliminary data that clearly supports the proposed approach and its viability.
score_descriptors.5: Compelling preliminary data that convincingly demonstrates feasibility and strengthens the rationale.

sub_criteria.name: Team Qualification
sub_criteria.description: The qualifications, expertise, and track record of the PI and research team to carry out the proposed work.

score_descriptors.1: Team lacks relevant expertise. No demonstrated ability to carry out the proposed work.
score_descriptors.2: Team has limited relevant experience. Significant skill gaps are evident.
score_descriptors.3: Team has adequate qualifications but some expertise gaps. Track record is modest.
score_descriptors.4: Team is well-qualified with strong relevant expertise and a solid publication record.
score_descriptors.5: Team is exceptionally qualified with leading expertise, outstanding track record, and complementary skills.

broader_impacts.name: Broader Impacts
broader_impacts.description: The Broader Impacts criterion encompasses the potential to benefit society and contribute to the achievement of specific, desired societal outcomes.

sub_criteria.name: Societal Benefit
sub_criteria.description: The potential for the research to produce tangible benefits for society, the economy, public health, or national security.

score_descriptors.1: No societal benefit articulated or the connection is entirely implausible.
score_descriptors.2: Vague or unconvincing societal benefits. Claims are not well-supported.
score_descriptors.3: Some societal benefits identified but the pathway from research to impact is unclear.
score_descriptors.4: Clear and credible societal benefits with a plausible pathway to realization.
score_descriptors.5: Compelling societal benefits with a well-articulated and realistic plan for achieving real-world impact.

sub_criteria.name: Education Integration
sub_criteria.description: The degree to which the project integrates research with education, training, and mentoring activities.

score_descriptors.1: No educational component. Research and education are completely disconnected.
score_descriptors.2: Minimal educational integration. Token or superficial training activities.
score_descriptors.3: Some educational activities planned but they are generic or loosely connected to the research.
score_descriptors.4: Strong integration of research and education with clear training opportunities for students.
score_descriptors.5: Exceptional educational plan deeply woven into the research with innovative pedagogical approaches.

sub_criteria.name: Diversity and Inclusion
sub_criteria.description: Plans to broaden participation of underrepresented groups in STEM through the proposed activities.

score_descriptors.1: No attention to diversity or inclusion. No plans to broaden participation.
score_descriptors.2: Diversity is mentioned but no concrete plans or strategies are provided.
score_descriptors.3: Some diversity efforts planned but they lack specificity or institutional commitment.
score_descriptors.4: Well-developed diversity plan with specific strategies, partnerships, and measurable goals.
score_descriptors.5: Outstanding and innovative diversity plan with demonstrated institutional support and evidence-based strategies.

sub_criteria.name: Dissemination Plan
sub_criteria.description: The strategy for sharing results, data, and tools with the broader scientific community and the public.

score_descriptors.1: No dissemination plan. Results would remain siloed.
score_descriptors.2: Dissemination limited to standard publications with no broader outreach.
score_descriptors.3: Reasonable dissemination plan including publications and some data sharing.
score_descriptors.4: Strong dissemination plan with open data, community engagement, and multi-channel outreach.
score_descriptors.5: Exemplary dissemination strategy including open-source tools, public engagement, media outreach, and policy briefs.

sub_criteria.name: Infrastructure Enhancement
sub_criteria.description: The potential to enhance research infrastructure such as facilities, instrumentation, networks, or partnerships.

score_descriptors.2: Marginal infrastructure enhancement with limited lasting value.
score_descriptors.3: Some infrastructure benefits such as shared datasets or minor facility improvements.
score_descriptors.4: Meaningful infrastructure contributions that will benefit the broader research community.
score_descriptors.5: Transformative infrastructure enhancement creating lasting shared resources, facilities, or collaborative networks.
